title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear an organiser talking at a language exchange club.
audio_script: Speaker: Thank you for coming to the first meeting of the language exchange club. The club is for people who want to practise speaking another language in a friendly place. You don't need to be perfect, and nobody will laugh if you make mistakes. From next week, our meetings will be in the [14]college café[/14], because it is bigger than this room and open in the evening. We meet every Thursday from seven until nine. The first half hour is for introductions, and after that we sit in small groups. This month, the most popular language is [15]Spanish[/15], but we also have groups for French, German and Italian. If you want to practise a different language, please write it on the list by the door. New members often feel nervous at first, but after a few minutes most people relax and start talking. We change groups during the evening, so you will not spend the whole meeting with the same people. Each week, one member will give a short talk. The first talk is about [16]music festivals[/16], and it should be easy to understand. Please bring a pen, as we often play word games and write down useful phrases. You may also bring a [17]dictionary[/17], but using your phone all the time is not a good idea.  Please speak clearly and slowly, because the aim is practice, not speed. Drinks are not free, but tea costs only [18]one pound[/18]. Finally, if you want club news, join our online group. The password for this week is [19]orange[/19]. I'll put it on the noticeboard before you leave.

questions.explanation: The speaker says meetings will be in the college café from next week.
The speaker says the most popular language this month is Spanish.
The first short talk is about music festivals.
The speaker says members may bring a dictionary.
The speaker gives orange as the online group password.
